PDP Buy Box — Before → After

Today the only action is a single "ADD TO BAG". This adds an express path, live demand proof, the UPI offer surfaced at the buy box, a delivery date, and post-add direction. Fit signals are intentionally left out — that's already covered in F3.

Live site stops here: one CTA, no express checkout, no delivery date, the UPI offer only appears inside GoKwik after you reach checkout, and nothing tells you what to do after adding.
New finding · round 2 · N6

One slow path, and the offer is hidden until checkout

aNo express / Buy-Now path

"Add to Bag" forces cart → drawer → checkout. A returning, decided buyer has no 1-tap route. Express wallets (UPI / GPay / PayLater) at the buy box shorten the highest-intent journey.

bUPI offer surfaces too late

The "extra discount on UPI" only appears inside GoKwik at checkout. Surfacing "pay ₹2,089 with UPI" at the buy box both lifts perceived value and pre-steers COD→prepaid before the decision is made.

cNo live demand proof at the CTA

14,744 reviews exist, but the buy box is static. A real "X bought in 24h" / "trending" signal next to the CTA is social proof exactly where it converts. (Must be real counts — fits the honesty brand.)

dNo delivery date & no post-add direction

No "delivery by <date>" at the buy box, and after adding, the shopper gets no clear next step (keep shopping vs checkout vs build the B3G1 bundle).

Combined hypothesis: an express checkout + surfaced UPI offer + live demand proof + delivery date shortens the path and raises intent → ↑ PDP→order CR, ↑ prepaid share, ↓ steps-to-purchase. Metrics: PDP→order CR, prepaid %, express-CTA usage. Ship as isolated A/B tests so you can attribute each lever.
Effort · Low–MedImpact · High
